raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.7, "R1.2": 0.7, "R1.3": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.5": 0.3, "R1.6": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.3, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.1": "The section advances a contribution claim (a novel probing protocol + above-chance self-recognition correlated with self-preference); any opinion on the work requires reconstructing whether that thesis is coherent and well-grounded.", "R1.2": "The protocol has internal logical dependencies (pairing construction → evaluator role → position-bias control → accuracy metric) that must hold together; checking their consistency is central to any scientific read.", "R1.3": "Accuracy values (0.58–0.81) and the correlation with self-preference are stated as empirical claims but no tables or figures are present in the excerpt; verifying that the prose claims are plausibly supported is a core expected responsibility.", "R1.4": "An open-ended 'what do you think?' on a research methodology section almost certainly includes a judgment about whether the protocol and findings represent a meaningful advance over prior evaluator-bias literature.", "R1.6": "The passage has marked anaphoric repetition ('self-recognition accuracy' appears ~8 times, 'we record' 3 times, 'our protocol' doubled in one sentence); writing quality is an obvious and expected target of any informal opinion request."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Are you asking for a scientific or methodological critique (logical soundness, evidence quality, novelty relative to prior work), or primarily for writing feedback (concision, clarity, prose style)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section advances a contribution claim (a novel probing protocol + above-chance self-recognition correlated with self-preference); any opinion on the work requires reconstructing whether that thesis is coherent and well-grounded.
rationale.R1.2: The protocol has internal logical dependencies (pairing construction → evaluator role → position-bias control → accuracy metric) that must hold together; checking their consistency is central to any scientific read.
rationale.R1.3: Accuracy values (0.58–0.81) and the correlation with self-preference are stated as empirical claims but no tables or figures are present in the excerpt; verifying that the prose claims are plausibly supported is a core expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: An open-ended 'what do you think?' on a research methodology section almost certainly includes a judgment about whether the protocol and findings represent a meaningful advance over prior evaluator-bias literature.
rationale.R1.6: The passage has marked anaphoric repetition ('self-recognition accuracy' appears ~8 times, 'we record' 3 times, 'our protocol' doubled in one sentence); writing quality is an obvious and expected target of any informal opinion request.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a scientific or methodological critique (logical soundness, evidence quality, novelty relative to prior work), or primarily for writing feedback (concision, clarity, prose style)?
